sections.content: The board changed after clicking on the charcoal object. Let me compare what changed between the previous frame and current frame to understand the effect of my click.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after the click
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}

print("BEFORE nodes:")
for n in before['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']}")

print("\nAFTER nodes:")
for n in after['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']}")

# Find differences
before_hashes = {n['hash'] for n in before['nodes']}
after_hashes = {n['hash'] for n in after['nodes']}
print("\nNew hashes:", after_hashes - before_hashes)
print("Removed hashes:", before_hashes - after_hashes)

# Check pixel count changes
print("\nPixel changes:")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['hash'] in before_hashes:
        bn = [x for x in before['nodes'] if x['hash'] == n['hash']][0]
        if bn['pixels'] != n['pixels']:
            print(f"  hash={n['hash']} color={n['color']} {bn['pixels']} -> {n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
